page: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 40 vCPUs mapped from 20 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is configured at 160.0 GB, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 768 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves a top-tier rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance with a score of 3083.57 and a strong rating in multi-core performance at 123693.48. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read-only operations reaching 451.84 GB/sec and write operations reaching 262.45 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Passmark CPU tests yield a strong score of 72888.75, with strong results across extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. For database and caching workloads, the system demonstrates strong performance with 84.31 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, while Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced general-purpose profile. The shared CPU allocation model represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, though the high core count and fast NVMe storage mitigate resource contention. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded processing, medium-scale database hosting, and web serving. It is well-suited for workloads such as Redis caching, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate LLM inference, where it achieves 35.09 tokens per second for text generation on a 7B model.
description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 40 shared vCPUs from 20 physical AMD EPYC cores, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core execution and Redis SET throughput of 84.31 million operations per second. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained peak processing, the high core density and fast local storage provide qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive applications. This server is suited for multi-threaded data processing, caching layers, database hosting, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 40 shared vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 768 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd features 40 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB NVMe storage, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core server featuring high-capacity NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 40 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 160 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
768 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 451.84 GB/sec large-block read speeds
Strong multi-core performance and 84.31 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing
